title: Transponder, transponder assembly, transceiver, monitoring system, method of installation and kit for obtaining sensor data related to an idler roller of a belt conveyor system
abstract: Disclosed is a transponder, transponder assembly, transceiver, monitoring system, method of installation, and kit for obtaining sensor data related to an idler roller of a belt conveyor system. The transponder is mountable to the idler roller and includes a wireless communication device and a temperature sensor. The microcontroller is configured to obtain a temperature measurement indicative of a temperature of a bearing of the idler roller, and transmit sensor data indicative of the temperature to a transceiver. The transceiver includes a second wireless communication device and a data bus transceiver. The second microcontroller is configured to: receive the sensor data transmitted by the transponder; process the sensor data to determine if an alert condition has been satisfied; in response to the alert condition being satisfied, transfer, via the data bus transceiver of the microcontroller to a diagnostic processing system, an alert and/or at least some of the sensor data.
claims: Claims The claims defining the invention are as follows: 1. A transponder for generating sensor data related to an idler roller of a belt conveyor system, wherein the transponder is mountable to the idler roller and includes a wireless communication device and a temperature sensor integrated with or coupled to a microcontroller configured to obtain, via the temperature sensor, a temperature measurement indicative of a temperature of a bearing of the idler roller, and transmit, via the wireless communication device, sensor data indicative of the temperature to a transceiver. 2. The transponder according to claim 1, wherein the sensing device is a passive device which is electrically powered based on a wireless signal received from the transceiver which is an active device. 3. The transponder according to claim 2, wherein the sensing device communicates with the transceiver utilising Near Field Communication (NFC). 4. The transponder according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the sensing device includes an accelerometer coupled to the microcontroller, wherein at least some of the sensor data transmitted to the transceiver is indicative of rotational and/or vibration signals sensed by the accelerometer. 5. The transponder according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the transponder includes an acoustic sensor coupled to the microcontroller, wherein at least some of the sensor data transmitted to the transceiver is indicative of acoustic signals sensed by the acoustic sensor. 6. The transponder according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the sensing device includes a vibration sensor coupled to the microcontroller, wherein at least some of the sensor data transmitted to the transceiver is indicative of vibration sensed by the vibration sensor. 7. The transponder according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the transponder has a ring shaped body including a central aperture to receive therethrough a shaft of the idler roller. 8. The transponder according to claim 7, wherein the body is a circuit board. 9. A transponder assembly including: an adaptor boss configured to retrofittably engage or be secured to a mechanical seal of an idler roller; and a transponder configured according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the transponder is secured to the adaptor boss. 10. The transponder assembly according to claim 9, wherein the mechanical seal is a labyrinth seal of the idler roller. 11. The transponder assembly according to claim 9 or 10, wherein the adaptor boss and transponder rotate together with a roller element of the idler roller. 12. A monitoring system for obtaining sensor data related to an idler roller of a belt conveyor system, wherein the monitoring system includes: a transponder configured according to any one of claims 1 to 8; and the transceiver including a second wireless communication device and a data bus transceiver integrated with or coupled to a second microcontroller configured to: receive, via the second wireless communication device, the sensor data transmitted by the transponder; process the sensor data to determine if an alert condition has been satisfied; in response to the alert condition being satisfied, transfer, via the data bus transceiver of the microcontroller to a diagnostic processing system, at least one of: an alert; and at least some of the sensor data. 13. The monitoring system according to claim 12, wherein the monitoring system includes a plurality of transponders and a plurality of transceivers. 14. A monitoring system for obtaining sensor data related to an idler roller of a belt conveyor system, wherein the monitoring system includes: a transponder assembly configured according to any one of claims 9 to 11; and the transceiver including a second wireless communication device and a data bus transceiver integrated with or coupled to a second microcontroller configured to: receive, via the second wireless communication device, the sensor data transmitted by the transponder; process the sensor data to determine if an alert condition has been satisfied; in response to the alert condition being satisfied, transfer, via the data bus transceiver of the second microcontroller to a diagnostic processing system, at least one of: an alert; and at least some of the sensor data. 15. The monitoring system according to claim 14, including a dust cover to retrofittably secure to the idler roller to cover the transponder and the adaptor boss. 16. The monitoring system according to claim 15, wherein the cover includes a central aperture. 17. The monitoring system according to claim 15 or 16, wherein the adaptor boss and transponder rotate together under the cover. 18. The monitoring system according to any one of claims 14 to 17, wherein the transceiver is mounted to a bracket of the idler roller. 19. The monitoring system according to any one of claims 15 to 17, wherein the transceiver, or a portion thereof, is mounted to the dust cover. 20. The monitoring system according to any one of claims 14 to 19, wherein the monitoring system includes a plurality of transponder assemblies and a plurality of transceivers. 21. A belt conveyor system including: a plurality of idler rollers supporting a conveyor belt; and a monitoring system configured according to any one of claims 12 to 20. 22. A method of installing a monitoring system to an idler roller, wherein the monitoring system is configured according to claim 14, wherein the method includes: removing an end cap of the idler roller; mounting the adaptor boss to a mechanical seal of the idler roller; securing the transponder to a mounting surface of the adaptor boss; mounting the transceiver relative to the transponder; and connecting the transceiver to a data link to communicate with a diagnostic processing system. 23. The method according to claim 22, wherein the method includes mounting the transceiver to a bracket of the idler roller. 24. The method according to claim 23, wherein the method includes mounting a dust cover to an end cap engagement assembly of the idler roller. 25. The method according to claim 22, wherein the transceiver, or a portion thereof, is mounted to a dust cover, wherein the step of mounting the transceiver is at least partially performed by mounting the dust cover to an end cap engagement assembly of the idler roller. 26. A kit for obtaining sensor data related to an idler roller of a belt conveyor system, wherein the kit includes: a transponder assembly configured according to any one of claims 9 to 11; and a transceiver including a second wireless communication device and a data bus transceiver integrated with or coupled to a second microcontroller configured to: receive, via the second wireless communication device, the sensor data transmitted by the transponder; process the sensor data to determine if an alert condition has been satisfied; in response to the alert condition being satisfied, transfer, via the data bus transceiver of the microcontroller to a diagnostic processing system, at least one of: an alert; and at least some of the sensor data. 27. The kit according to claim 26, wherein the kit includes a dust cover to retrofittably securable to the idler roller to cover the transponder and the adaptor boss. 28. The kit according to claim 27, wherein the cover includes a central aperture. 29. The kit according to claim 27 or 28, wherein the adaptor boss and transponder rotate together under the cover. 30. The kit according to any one of claims 26 to 29, wherein the transceiver is mountable to a bracket of the idler roller. 31. The kit according to any one of claims 27 to 29, wherein the transceiver, or a portion thereof, is mounted to the dust cover.
